posts.text: Latest projections here: natesilver.net/p/2026-march-…

🏀 Took a deeper look at injury adjustments in our NCAA model tonight and found they were probably too aggressive. We'd always rather make the best projections we can. Michigan and Duke, which have more injuries, are helped by this, creating a ~3-way tie up top.

More analysis and all our numbers here. And you can find a link to our men's projections too, which we published late last night ICYMI.

natesilver.net/p/2026-womens…

The #1s are typically more dominant in women's hoops than for the men, but that's especially true this year. Some very, very good programs at the top. Big step down after that, except to some extent LSU which is kind of an unfair matchup for UCLA in the Elite Eight.

Our women's March Madness projections are ready! 🏀🏀🏀

1. UConn 35%
2. Texas 22%
3. South Carolina 21%
4. UCLA 17%
5. LSU 4%

The difference between public perceptions and gambler perceptions is funny sometimes. Miami (OH) is 31-1, widely perceived to have been robbed of a higher seed, but 7½-point underdogs in their First Four game against SMU according to Vegas. Our model has it a *bit* closer FWIW.

We love doing these every year, and we've put in a lot of work on both the frontend and the backend to hopefully make them better than ever. Bookmark this page, as we'll be updating the odds every day.
natesilver.net/p/2026-march-…

Our MARCH MADNESS projections are ready! 🏀🏀🏀

And in a bit of a surprise, Arizona is the very narrow favorite over Duke and Michigan, who are probably better teams but have tougher draws and far more injury issues.
natesilver.net/p/cooper-mens…

Final pre-tournament COOPER ratings.

Duke and Michigan have been trading the #1 slot back and forth, but Duke has a somewhat clear lead there after Michigan lost today. Link below, tournament projections coming later tonight!

Other than that, Mrs. Lincoln, how was the play?


Ryan Petersen (@typesfast)

Guys, relax, the only risk in transiting the Strait of Hormuz is that Iran will shoot a missile at your ship. Otherwise it's open for transit. 

An all-time quote from our Secretary of War.

Video
  





— https://nitter.net/typesfast/status/2032469361748046024#m

Michigan #1 now in COOPER after Duke's close call last night. These are not adjusted for injuries, although that will hurt Duke a little bit too when we add them for our tournament forecast.
natesilver.net/p/cooper-mens…

From a purely electoral standpoint (not that I don't have opinions about it from a non-electoral standpoint) Iran has to be one of the dumber moves in recent memory. Maybe the base case is that things get back to normal, but the risks for Trump are weighted to the downside.
natesilver.net/p/cooper-wome…

Our women's COOPER ratings just dropped! (They are co-named after Cooper Flagg and Cynthia Cooper, after all.) Way more dominance than in the men's game. NCAA tournament forecasts to follow after the brackets are announced.

You can check my Twitter feed for the prominent names I was fighting with about this over the weekend (a big VC and a TV host). But more importantly, people in the White House and the Defense Department seemed to be engaged in exactly the same sort of magical thinking.


Varad Mehta (@varadmehta)

"Fuck those people who said it would all be over in a few days."

So fuck a bunch of Twitter randos? That's a weird thing to say. Anyone know why Big Nate is crashing out so hard over this? Just seems

It's a war so I'm gonna be very nice and not dunk on people who 100% deserve it but honestly fuck those people who said it would all be over in a few days.


Joe Weisenthal (@TheStalwart)

Oil tanker crossings through the Strait of Hormuz





— https://nitter.net/TheStalwart/status/2032103844759384211#m

13 thoughts on Anthropic, OpenAI and the Department of War
Who’s the real favorite in the Texas Senate primary? (by Eli McKown-Dawson). Nonpartisan polls give Crockett the edge. So why are prediction markets so bullish on Talarico?
